entries.must_match_behavior: A validated donor project root can be ingested into canonical project/novel/scene objects and optional chapter_artifact snapshots while preserving donor provenance for every imported object.
entries.acceptable_delta: Superwriter may normalize donor IDs into new canonical object IDs and may ignore donor runtime services or databases after import completes.
entries.verification_check: Tests import a synthetic .webnovel/state.json fixture and assert canonical rows, derived rows, and import record metadata.
entries.sunset_criteria: Sunset this row when a stable official interchange format replaces direct donor state import for project bootstrap.

entries.must_match_behavior: Offline donor character exports can be revalidated and imported into canonical character objects linked to an existing novel context.
entries.acceptable_delta: Superwriter may drop donor-local storage shape, reuse no donor runtime code, and assign fresh canonical object IDs instead of donor IDs.
entries.verification_check: Tests import a synthetic characters.json fixture and assert canonical character provenance plus an import record with low-trust metadata.
entries.sunset_criteria: Sunset this row when low-trust decompiled exports are replaced by implementation-donor exports or a first-party interchange contract.

entries.must_match_behavior: Importer must remain input-only and must not depend on embeddings, vector writes, panel refresh callbacks, or workspace-local storage after import.
entries.acceptable_delta: All donor runtime side effects are explicitly rejected and recorded as non-goals rather than parity gaps.
entries.verification_check: Tests assert the lower-trust importer contract and parity matrix both encode forbidden runtime dependencies and explicit non-goals.
entries.sunset_criteria: Sunset this row when no migration path needs the decompiled donor as a concept or data source.
